security_proposition: A high-integrity destructive and anti-forensic command sequence was launched, including a confirmed Security-log clear.

attack_category.note: RESEARCHER-INTENT: evidence_condition = STRONG (Tier-2 fusion). Sysmon records high-integrity vssadmin, bcdedit, and wevtutil command invocations; Security Event 1102 independently confirms that the Security audit log was cleared and identifies the account. Success of shadow-copy deletion, BCD changes, and System-log clearing is not asserted without result telemetry. Do not leak to model.

tier.why_single_source: GENUINE MULTI-SENSOR FUSION: Sysmon (EID 1) carries the destructive commands; Security carries the 1102 log-clear event that Sysmon cannot see (and whose 4688 process audits were wiped by that very clear). Same host (win-dc-173), same 10:56 window. The Security log is stanza format, converted to XML by the engine.

boundary_definition: Sysmon EV = the two bcdedit recovery-disable commands and the two wevtutil log-clear commands (10:56:42).
Security EV = the single 1102 'audit log cleared' (security:324614, 10:56:42) — the only surviving Security event, recording the erasure.
Scoped to win-dc-tcontreras-attack-range-173; the other host (win-host-985) and routine domain traffic are excluded.
The destruction's Security 4688 audits are ABSENT because the attacker cleared the log — this absence is itself the point, and Sysmon supplies what Security lost.

main_alert_selection.selection_method: vssadmin delete shadows is a high-fidelity T1490 (inhibit recovery) destruction/ransomware detection.
main_alert_selection.selection_reason: It is the destructive trigger; the bcdedit/wevtutil commands and the Security 1102 confirm a destructive-wiper + anti-forensic attack.

event_groups.description: vssadmin shadow delete, bcdedit recovery disable, wevtutil clear System + Security.
1102 audit-log-cleared by Administrator — proves the Security log was wiped and names the actor.

A0.role_note: triggering alert: vssadmin delete shadows /all /quiet is launched; deletion success is not independently recorded

EV-001.role_note: Security Event 1102 confirms that the audit log was cleared and names the account

EV-002.role_note: bcdedit is launched to request bootstatuspolicy ignoreallfailures; result status is absent

EV-003.role_note: bcdedit is launched to request recoveryenabled no; result status is absent

EV-004.role_note: wevtutil cl System is launched; System-log clear success is not independently recorded

EV-005.role_note: wevtutil cl Security is launched at the time of the corroborating Security 1102 event

curation_notes.note: RESEARCHER-PRIVATE: STRONG / preserve (Tier-2). The destructive and anti-forensic command burst is corroborated by a confirmed Security-log clear; other requested outcomes remain bounded.

wevtutil cl Security (Sysmon) and the 1102 log-cleared (Security) are the SAME act seen from two sensors: Sysmon has the command, Security has the result + the account. The clear is why the destruction's own Security audits are gone and Sysmon is the sole witness.
The Security log is Splunk stanza (key=value) format; the engine converts it to XML on load. RecordNumber 324614 = EventID 1102.

preprocessing_required: project raw fields to neutral attributes

review_required.ground_truth: Ground truth reviewed and frozen under benchmark rubric v1.1.
